reviews.review_description: This game was not really a hyped and thrilling game. I would highly recommend getting a different game to spend your money on. This games has zero updates which is the reason the game's graphics are not that good. I would not play this game if it was free.
The controllers are not working
This is so fun its hard but fun buy it is so good this game is the goat!

If you've been looking for a solid dinosaur shooting game on the Meta Quest, this title might be the best one you'll find right now. The graphics are well-executed and the overall experience is more satisfying than many other dinosaur-themed shooters on the platform, which often suffer from glitchy gameplay and horrible visuals.

However, you need to go into this experience with one key piece of information in mind: this seems like a direct port of an arcade game. And as such, it is designed to take your money.

This arcade DNA is the game's biggest weakness. You'll quickly notice that your shots are sometimes delayed, discounted, or outright ignored, which feels incredibly frustrating. Tiny dinosaurs will sneak in from the very edges of your vision and before they even get within four feet of you, you'll be considered "bitten" and dead. To progress, you have to rely on memorization and repeatedly playing the same levels, because the game's design puts you at a distinct disadvantage.

If you can accept these intentional frustrations as part of the arcade design, you will find the game pleasurable to play for a while. I found myself enjoying a few rounds before the repetition began to kill the fun. The experience becomes more about beating the system's unfair rules than it is about an immersive, natural shooting experience.

Overall, it's a fun distraction that offers better visuals and core gameplay than its competitors. But the frustrating design choices meant to drain your wallet in an arcade setting hold it back on a home platform.

Rating: 3 out of 5 stars.

The game looks allright, but isn't really ballanced. Gunplay needs to be very precise. There's no room for error, which wouldn't be so bad, if you didn't need to memorize the waves of dinosaurs to be succesfull, instead of rely on skill.
And it still feels slow and boring.

Okay, this could easily be the best game I have, in my opinion which I would be willing to pay at least twice what I paid for it if not more, if there weren't just some things that are very annoying in which I can't believe they didn't put in there.

1. option to instantly replay a level once you fail instead of having to go back to the main menu every time just to go back to the same level.

2. option to just end a level when you're in the middle of it to either go back to the main menu or to just replay. Once you already know you failed the mission it's stupid to have to sit there and wait for the level to end for you to be able to exit or restart.

3.  a practice shooting range that's just a free shooter, not for points, not timed, nothing like that. Just be able to select a specific number of targets that will pop up, stand there and shoot at them, And to be able to practice with different types of weapons That you will eventually be able to use later in the game.

4. Aim calibration. Clearly aiming dead center and yet missing. And yet other targets I'm shooting at the exact same spot and knocking them down instantly. That has to be my biggest complaint. And the aim gets worse if you select the laser.

5. The dinosaurs are killing you before they even fully get to you. 

But the graphics are beautiful, for the most part the mechanics are really awesome. Not being able to freely walk is a little strange but done decent enough. With a few changes this would really be an awesome shooter, And I don't think it would require that much for these updates. You're getting your money, yes, but you could be making so much more.

Could be a cool game if it gave you ANY settings for difficulty and the dinosaurs didn't damage you from 5-feet away, ESPECIALLY considering you never know where they are coming from until you are already basically/ completely dead from getting 3 surprise hits whilst trying to find out where they are hitting you from!
I'm not even having luck getting past the first level because of this.
The developers do not even care to update the game or tighten it up either, so it's just not worth having/ keeping.

Reminds me of an  arcade game in which you just aim and shoot.  The only thing missing is a narrator.

Normally, I would request a refund  faster than I can say "refund."

Something about the nostalgia of the game.  I can get in fairly quickly, shoot some dinosaurs, and get out.
The graphics are nice, and the guns look good.  Wish I could select the other  ones at times. 

I am keeping this game in hopes that the developers will add a mode that allows me to play through the entire game without getting harmed.  

This game has so much potential with just a little tweaking  and a 10 dollar price tag, which is how I got it,  this would be a best seller.

I've been a big fan of games like Time Crisis or House of the Dead since I was young. Dinosaur Island has the same essence, but with dinosaurs and other animals from the past (titanoboas, giant mosquitoes...). It's lighthearted and easy to learn, which is very appreciated in VR for quick plays. In the Controls section, the game allows you to configure the weapons (my favorite are the double pistols), the laser sight for better aiming, left-handed mode... Good options to suit your style of play

reviews.review_title: Good mix of dinosaurs and shooting
reviews.review_description: Easy to learn & play. Interesting campaign mode, but the mission mode is better to start with. The experience and difficulty changes a lot depending on the weapon you choose. For example, the pistol has a very slow rate of fire and I have not felt comfortable, but choosing the machine gun, it's much better and closer to the experience I'm looking for, because the difficulty is more adjusted. There is a variety of levels (from a jungle to a concrete base) and enemies (mostly dinosaurs but there are other creatures). Worth the price.
It has a great feel when starting it, but unfortunately just not overly stimulating on the game play, only one hand works on the gun.
I can't even start this game won't work bad

I guess I just don't like arcade style shooters. You can't move around on your own. You're stuck in a track. The game is also difficult with little options to change anything or even select a different gun. The gun they give you has a ridiculously slow fire rate for a "semi-auto". I find this game frustrating and not fun. Not worth the $15 to me. This is probably more of a $10 game at best. Not horrible, but really not great. Would have been way more fun if you could move around on your own or at least have a better gun to start with. Also the sight is off.
Guys. This is great but it needs easier settings.  The dinosaurs come anywhere near you, you die instantly??   PLEASE ADD!!

Great graphics and atmosphere, a real Jurassic Park vibe, but oh my it's tough!  That would be fine if there was a changeable difficultly option but there isn't. Just a more effective gun at the beginning would help.  Please devs, if you could fix that it would be a winner!
The best game I've ever played on vr, it really gives you a good challenge and I like that . I'm successfully finished the game. I just wish they were more stages and more games like this.
When i am open game control not clicking. My control working fine but only when opem dinousr island menu click not working
I don't understand how come the game ends so fast while im trying to shoot

Game not bad,graphics very good the dinosaurs look really good ,game has.only one difficulty level and that is not easy, a lot of players might refund because of that….the gun sighting  seems to be a bit off ,found firing just beneath the red laser dot more successful. Loved this game on the old oculus go headset

I really like the design and how the game looks overall, graphic is really good!! All environments are beautiful as well as dinosaurs models. Of course there is things to improve but it's an app lab so no concerns. The biggest issue I have to the moment is that my character always hovers 2-3 meters above the ground. Do you have Discord by chance?

I just bought it and played several rounds. I'm already addicted to it.  It's easier in some ways and harder in some ways than Primal Hunt. 

Think JURASSIC PARK 3 and you're on it.
Gorgeous graphics and menus. A bit hard for me but very addictive. Recommended.
